title: Enabling checkpoint/restore testing on Fedora >= 29 again
body: Trying to enable checkpoint/restore and container migration in https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2272 fails with different (sometimes checkpoint/restore unrelated) errors. To make sure that this is not some side-effect of my code changes, I am using this PR to understand if and how my changes break the CI results.

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

bot, add author to whitelist
LGTM
If it passes the test

@rhatdan I see new SELinux errors locally when trying to re-create the errors here. These test errors do not show up in `ausearch`, but using `journalctl` I can see:
```
Mar 19 16:42:29 fedora29 audit: SELINUX_ERR op=security_bounded_transition seresult=denied oldcontext=unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 newcontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c218,c449
Mar 19 16:42:29 fedora29 audit: SELINUX_ERR op=security_bounded_transition seresult=denied oldcontext=unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 newcontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c218,c449
Mar 19 16:42:29 fedora29 audit: SELINUX_ERR op=security_bounded_transition seresult=denied oldcontext=unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 newcontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c218,c449
Mar 19 16:42:29 fedora29 audit: SELINUX_ERR op=security_bounded_transition seresult=denied oldcontext=unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 newcontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c218,c449
```

This seems to be CRIU trying to relabel each restored thread.
@rhatdan Do you have any ideas how solve those SELinux errors messages in the previous comment?
Nope, I really do not understand what is going on here.
It seems I have to change the process context before creating the threads in CRIU. I will report back here when I have more information.

@rhatdan One more question. The setcon() man page says:
>  A multi-threaded application can perform a setcon() prior to creating any child threads, in which case all of the child threads will inherit the new context.  However, prior to Linux 2.6.28, setcon() would fail if there are any other threads running in the same process since this would yield an inconsistency among the security contexts of threads sharing the same memory space.  Since Linux 2.6.28, setcon() is permitted for threads  within  a multi-threaded process if the new security context is bounded by the old security context, where the bounded relation is defined through typebounds statements in the policy and guarantees that the new security context has a subset of the permissions of the old security context.

So with a typebound policy it could probably work. If I understand this correctly container_t would need to be a subset of container_runtime_t. Looking at container-selinux I do not see typebound used very often, so there is no typebound relation between container_t and container_runtime_t. Is that correct?

Any chance that there will be a typebound relation between container_t and container_runtime_t?

My understanding of typebounds is that is not used much anymore.  We have moved towards using
```
	allow container_runtime_t contianer_t:process2 nnp_transition;
```
In the kernel.
Which rule already exists.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2833) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Needs a rebase.
Rebased, but not ready. Waiting on external dependencies.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3003) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Still missing updated SELinux policies to correctly restore threaded processes. Will update this PR once the policy exists.

Finally all necessary changes are available in the corresponding upstream packages and all tests are green. Right now I am still pulling a few packages directly from koji. I will remove [WIP] once the packages are available from the updates-released repository.
All necessary changes are now part of the criu, container-selinux and selinux-policy packages in Fedora >= 29. I removed the [WIP] flag as it is ready for merge.
Any chance getting this merged? I could then continue with my other large PR #2272
LGTM and happy green tests.
/lgtm
If I understand the process correctly this PR now only needs the approved label, right? Is there anything missing to get it approved?
@adrianreber I think we just missed adding it, will do so.
/approved
/approve
